description_long: This object is a classic submachine gun, specifically resembling the Thompson M1928, often known as the 'Tommy Gun.' It features a long, ribbed barrel with a prominent cooling fin design and a large, round drum magazine mounted beneath the receiver. The stock is made of dark wood and is fixed, extending smoothly from the rear of the receiver. The foregrip is also wooden and has a vertical, contoured design for improved handling. The receiver and barrel assembly are metallic, with a matte black finish, and the iron sights are integrated into the top of the receiver and barrel. The overall design is iconic, with a blend of wood and metal materials, and is historically associated with early 20th-century urban environments.
description: A classic Thompson-style submachine gun with a ribbed barrel, large drum magazine, and wooden fixed stock and foregrip. The body is primarily matte black metal with dark wood accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular drum magazine and the ribbed barrel extending forward.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the long ribbed barrel, large drum magazine, and wooden fixed stock and foregrip.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the width of the wooden stock and the circular drum magazine.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the same key features: ribbed barrel, drum magazine, and wooden stock.
